  • Windows Server 2012: KB5099445

Note: With recent and upcoming Windows updates over the next few months, a limited number of consumer and business devices might experience one additional restart during installation. This one‑time restart occurs after a Secure Boot certificate update is applied as part of the Secure Boot update process.

title: Enforcement phase for Kerberos RC4 protections begins with the July 2026 Windows security update

body.content:
What and why
Windows updates released in July 2026 introduce the enforcement phase of protections designed to address a Kerberos information disclosure vulnerability (CVE‑2026‑20833). This phase completes the transition away from RC4-based Kerberos tickets by removing Audit mode and leaving Enforcement mode as the only supported behavior for Kerberos RC4 usage on Windows domain controllers.

With the July 2026 security update installed, domain controllers enforce updated behavior for service account ticket issuance. RC4-based Kerberos tickets are restricted under this enforcement model, and AES-based encryption is expected for supported configurations. This change helps reduce reliance on legacy encryption methods and aligns Kerberos authentication with current security standards.

Rollout schedule
This change is effective beginning with the July 2026 security update.

Impact on your organization
Organizational environments that still depend on RC4-based Kerberos service tickets might experience authentication failures when requesting service tickets after installing the July 2026 security update.

Applications, services, or devices that rely on legacy encryption configurations or explicitly defined RC4 usage are most likely to be affected. Environments previously operating in Audit mode no longer have the ability to revert to that mode once enforcement is in place.

Workloads that previously completed Kerberos authentication without updated encryption configurations might no longer function as expected.

Action required/recommendations
  • Confirm that all service accounts and dependent applications support AES-based Kerberos encryption.
  • Review and remediate any remaining RC4 dependencies identified during prior audit phases.
  • Monitor the System event log for Kerberos-related events to identify authentication failures or misconfigured accounts.
  • Validate interoperability with non-Windows Kerberos implementations and services.
  • Review How to manage Kerberos KDC usage of RC4 for service account ticket issuance changes related to CVE‑2026‑20833.

Audit mode and the RC4DefaultDisablementPhase rollback setting are removed beginning with the July 2026 security update. If RC4 must be retained for specific services, explicit configuration is still possible, but it leaves those scenarios vulnerable to CVE‑2026‑20833 and should be used only as a temporary measure.

Compliance considerations
No compliance considerations are identified. Review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Finance and operations apps - Empower ERP users with Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to empower ERP users with Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat, combining ERP and workplace intelligence for fast decisions in Finance and operations apps. This feature will reach public preview on July 27, 2026.

How does this affect me?
This feature enables users to navigate, understand, and act on ERP data using natural language. The experience combines ERP intelligence from Dynamics 365 with workplace intelligence from Microsoft 365. Users can connect financial tasks with the broader business context from emails, meetings, documents, and Teams conversations, to resolve issues faster and make more informed decisions.

Copilot also accelerates common Finance and operations workflows by retrieving balances, summarizing vouchers, highlighting anomalies, tracing document chains, and identifying approval blockers. The assistant respects security roles and organizational permissions, providing accurate answers grounded in live ERP data.

What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

title: Network best practices for Teams town halls and live events with third‑party providers

details.name: Summary
details.value: To prevent streaming issues in Teams town halls and live events, review network settings, especially if using third-party secure web gateways, proxies, or traffic inspection. Optimize policies for real-time media, validate endpoint allowlists, configure VPN split-tunnel properly, test events, and prepare support teams. Updates ongoing.

body.content:
[What and Why:]

To help prevent streaming quality issues in Teams town halls and Teams live events, we recommend that you review your network configuration—especially if users connect through a secure web gateway (SWG), proxy, or traffic inspection solution (for example, Netskope, Zscaler, or Symantec).

[Rollout Schedule:]

This is an active issue. We are continuing to investigate and provide guidance to help reduce the risk of streaming quality issues. Updates will be shared as they become available.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Microsoft 365 tenants that run Teams town halls and/or Teams live events, especially where users connect through a third-party SWG, proxy, or traffic inspection provider.

Platforms/Services:

  • Microsoft Teams (town hall and Teams live events) across desktop, web, and mobile, depending on how attendees join and how your network routes traffic.

What will happen:

  • If your SWG, proxy, or traffic inspection policies aren’t optimized for real-time media, town halls and live events may experience:
    • Intermittent join or playback issues
    • Lower video quality or buffering
    • Increased latency or broadcast delay
  • If your network is already configured to allow Teams real-time media traffic as recommended, no change is expected.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

To help reduce the risk of streaming quality issues, review the following recommendations:

  • Validate Microsoft 365 endpoint allowlists and routing.
  • If you are using a VPN split-tunnel, ensure it is configured according to Implementing VPN split-tunnel.
  • Review your SWG, proxy, and inspection policies for Teams media traffic. Refer to Understanding implications when using network intermediation to decrypt or manipulate Microsoft 365 traffic at the network layer. Network intermediation is not recommended or supported for this scenario.
  • Run a test town hall or live events through the same path users will take in production.
  • Prepare your helpdesk and event organizers with troubleshooting guidance and diagnostic collection steps.

[Compliance considerations:]

No compliance considerations identified, review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Planned Windows 365 Connectivity Maintenance and RDP Multipath with Redundant TCP Transport Paths General Availability

details.name: Summary
details.value: Windows 365 will undergo planned connectivity maintenance from July 21-25, 2026, with no expected downtime. RDP Multipath with redundant TCP transport paths is now generally available, improving session resiliency. Users should run supported Windows App clients to minimize brief reconnections during maintenance.

body.content:
[Introduction]

To improve performance and reliability, we have scheduled planned maintenance for the Windows 365 service. This maintenance will occur during non-peak hours based on the user's local time zone to help minimize potential impact.

During this period, there will be no service downtime. Users are expected to experience little to no disruption.

We are also announcing that RDP Multipath with redundant TCP transport paths is now generally available for Windows 365 Cloud PCs. This capability improves session resiliency and helps reduce customer impact during connectivity maintenance events.

Historically, we provided notifications for routine TURN maintenance activities because they could result in user reconnections. With the general availability of RDP Multipath with redundant TCP transport paths, customers using supported configurations are expected to experience improved resiliency during these events, significantly reducing the potential impact of routine TURN maintenance activities.

As a result, Windows 365 expects to reduce the need for customer communications related to future routine TURN maintenance events. Customers will continue to be notified when a maintenance activity is expected to have customer impact or requires customer awareness.

[How this will affect your organization:]

Key Takeaways

  • Planned Windows 365 connectivity maintenance will occur between July 21, 2026, and July 25, 2026.
  • RDP Multipath with redundant TCP transport paths is now generally available.
  • Organizations using supported Windows App clients are expected to benefit from improved resiliency during connectivity maintenance events.
  • With the general availability of RDP Multipath with redundant TCP transport paths, Windows 365 expects to reduce the need for communications related to future routine TURN maintenance events.
  • To take advantage of redundant TCP transport paths, users must connect from a Windows device using Windows App on Windows version 2.0.1069.0 or later.
  • Customers using older client versions or environments where RDP Multipath prerequisites are not met may still experience brief automatic reconnections during the maintenance window.
  • No service downtime is expected.

Maintenance Schedule

Start Date

July 21, 2026

End Date

July 25, 2026

Daily Maintenance Window Start Times
  • Africa: 23:00 UTC
  • Asia Pacific: 16:30 UTC
  • Australia: 14:00 UTC
  • Europe: 00:00 UTC
  • India: 19:30 UTC
  • Middle East: 22:00 UTC
  • North America: 06:00 UTC
  • South America: 05:00 UTC
Daily Maintenance Window End Times
  • Africa: 03:00 UTC
  • Asia Pacific: 20:30 UTC
  • Australia: 18:00 UTC
  • Europe: 04:00 UTC
  • India: 23:30 UTC
  • Middle East: 02:00 UTC
  • North America: 10:00 UTC
  • South America: 09:00 UTC
  • Windows 365 will remain available throughout the maintenance period.
  • While the maintenance window spans up to four hours, reconnections are expected to occur only one or two times and will not persist throughout the entire maintenance window.
  • Organizations using supported Windows App clients are expected to benefit from improved resiliency during this maintenance event.
  • Customers using older client versions or environments where RDP Multipath prerequisites are not met may be more likely to experience brief reconnections.
  • Users can establish new connections without impact during the maintenance window.
  • No service downtime is expected.

[What you need to do to prepare:]

  • No further action is required, as systems will automatically recover from temporary interruptions.
  • We recommend ensuring users are running supported and current Windows App clients to benefit from the latest connectivity improvements and resiliency enhancements.
  • You may wish to notify your helpdesk and end users of the scheduled maintenance window and the possibility of brief automatic reconnections.
  • We appreciate your partnership as we continue improving the reliability and resiliency of Windows 365 connectivity.
  • For more information, see the Windows 365 RDP Multipath documentation .

title: Microsoft Forms automated email sender domain is changing

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft Forms will change its automated email sender from maccount@microsoft.com to no-reply@forms.mail.microsoft starting mid-August 2026. Admins should update mail flow rules and safe sender lists accordingly. GCC environments get this update from early September 2026. No compliance issues identified.

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Microsoft Forms will update the email address used for automated notifications from maccount@microsoft.com to no-reply@forms.mail.microsoft. This email address is used for Microsoft Forms automated emails, including response receipts, email notifications for new responses, and Forms response monitoring updates.

For GCC, GCC High, and DoD environments, admins will now have the option to configure notification emails. These notifications will also use no-reply@forms.mail.microsoft, aligning with Worldwide environments.

Bring your own domain (BYOD) email addresses remain supported when configured by a tenant admin. 

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • General Availability (Worldwide): Rollout begins in mid-August 2026 and is expected to complete by early September 2026.
  • General Availability (GCC, GCC High, DoD): Rollout begins in early September 2026 and is expected to complete by mid-September 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Organizations using Microsoft Forms automated email notifications.
  • Organizations with mail flow rules, mailbox rules, or filtering policies that reference maccount@microsoft.com.

Platforms/Services:

  • Microsoft Forms
  • Exchange Online mail flow
  • Email filtering and anti-spam solutions

What will happen:

  • Microsoft Forms automated emails will be sent from no-reply@forms.mail.microsoft instead of maccount@microsoft.com.
  • Response receipts will use the new sender address.
  • Email notifications for new responses will use the new sender address.
  • Forms response monitoring updates will use the new sender address.
  • Admins will be able to configure notification emails that use the new sender address.
  • Existing mailbox rules, mail flow rules, or filtering policies that reference the previous sender address may no longer function as intended.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

  • Review mail flow rules, mailbox rules, and filtering policies that reference maccount@microsoft.com.
  • Update any configurations that depend on the previous sender address.
  • Consider adding no-reply@forms.mail.microsoft to your organization's safe sender or allowed sender lists.
  • Communicate this change to end users and help desk teams as appropriate.
  • Review notification features that use this sender address and determine whether configuration changes are needed.

Learn more: Select the domain to use for email from Microsoft 365 products | Microsoft Learn.

[Compliance Considerations:]

No compliance considerations identified. Review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Microsoft SharePoint: Restricted content discovery enhancements for Copilot and Microsoft 365 search

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft is enhancing SharePoint's Restricted Content Discovery (RCD) to prevent content from RCD-enabled sites appearing in Copilot and Microsoft 365 search, improving data control and compliance. Changes roll out worldwide in late July 2026, requiring no immediate action but recommending policy reviews and user communication.

body.content:
[What and why]

We are enhancing Restricted Content Discovery (RCD) for SharePoint to give organizations stronger control over how content is surfaced in Copilot and Microsoft 365 search. These updates help reduce the risk of oversharing while ensuring AI-powered experiences respect organizational policies and support secure and compliant use of enterprise AI.

[Rollout schedule]

General availability (Worldwide, GCC High, DoD): We will begin rolling out in late July 2026 and expect to complete in late July 2026.

[Impact on your organization]

Who is affected

  •  SharePoint administrators managing RCD policies and users accessing content through Copilot and Microsoft 365 search

Platforms/services

  • SharePoint (web)
  • Microsoft 365 search
  • Copilot (including search and chat experiences)

What will happen

  • Files recently accessed by a user will no longer appear in Copilot or Microsoft 365 search when governed by RCD policy.
  • Recently accessed includes file-level activity such as viewing, editing, or sharing files.
  • AI-powered entry points will be removed from SharePoint sites with RCD enabled. Users will not see Copilot entry points such as the Copilot button, AI menus, or Create pages with AI. The Create an agent action already follows this behavior.
  • These changes apply only to SharePoint sites where Restricted Content Discovery (RCD) policy is enabled.
  • Existing RCD policies continue to apply with no configuration changes required.
  • Users will no longer see certain files on SharePoint sites with RCD enabled. This will change how users discover content and access Copilot capabilities on those sites.
  • The enhancement is applied automatically.

[Action required / recommendations]

No immediate action is required. However, you may consider the following:

  • Review current RCD policies.
  • Review sites where RCD is enabled to understand where Copilot functionality will not be available.
  • Inform helpdesk teams about expected changes to content visibility and Copilot entry points.
  • Update internal documentation if needed.
  • Evaluate additional use of RCD for governance and oversharing scenarios.

Learn more: Documentation will be updated closer to full rollout.

  • Restrict discovery of SharePoint sites and content | SharePoint in Microsoft 365 | SharePoint | Microsoft Learn

[Compliance considerations]

QuestionAnswer
Does the change alter how existing customer data is processed, stored, or accessed?Yes. Content from RCD-enabled sites will no longer be surfaced in Copilot or Microsoft 365 search.
Does the change introduce or significantly modify AI/ML or agent capabilities that interact with customer data?Yes. Copilot behavior is modified by preventing access to content from RCD-enabled sites.
Does the change alter how users can access their data?Yes. Users cannot access RCD-protected content through Copilot or search surfaces.
Does the change include an admin control and can it be controlled through Entra ID group membership?Yes. Behavior is controlled through existing RCD policy.

title: Copilot Studio - Share autonomous agents to end users

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to share autonomous agents with end users in Copilot Studio. This feature will reach public preview on July 30, 2026.

How does this affect me?
With this feature, you can create triggers for autonomous agents that run using end-user credentials. This enables agents to operate fully in the background, respond to external events, and be shared with end users so the agents can run on their behalf.

In addition, triggers are now a fully native capability within Copilot Studio. You can create, configure, test, update, and delete triggers directly inside Copilot Studio and manage the entire trigger lifecycle end-to-end. Existing triggers built on Power Automate will continue to function as they do today.

What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

title: Power Automate - Automate PowerPoint presentations in desktop flows

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to automate PowerPoint presentations in desktop flows in Power Automate. This feature will be available in public preview on July 31, 2026.

How does this affect me?
With this feature, users will be able to use Power Automate for desktop actions to interact with PowerPoint files directly within desktop flows. Users will be able to launch PowerPoint, create and update presentations by adding slides, and save changes as part of automated workflows. This will allow presentation creation and editing to be included in end-to-end automation scenarios, reducing manual steps and helping maintain consistency across presentations.

What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

If you would like more information on this feature, please visit Automate PowerPoint presentations in desktop flows.

title: Dataverse - Enhance Dataverse record descriptions for Copilot /mention

We are announcing the ability to enhance Dataverse record descriptions for Copilot /mention in Dataverse. This feature will reach general availability on August 14, 2026.

How does this affect me?
With this feature, end users of Microsoft 365 Copilot will see improved Dataverse, Dynamic 365, and Power Apps record references in Context IQ with richer, more descriptive labels that include environment, app, and table information. This makes it easier to identify and select the correct records during Copilot interactions.

What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

If you would like more information on this feature, please visit Enhance Dataverse record descriptions for Copilot /mention.

Key functionality of this feature includes the ability to:

title: 90-Day Reminder: Windows 11, version 24H2 and Windows 10 LTSB 2016 reaching end of updates on October 13, 2026

body.content:
On October 13, 2026, Windows 11, version 24H2 Home and Pro editions, and Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB 2016 will reach end of updates. After this date, devices running these editions will no longer receive monthly security and non-security preview updates containing protections from the latest security threats.

We recommend updating devices running Windows 11, version 24H2 Home and Pro editions to Windows 11, version 25H2 to continue receiving monthly security and quality updates. Enterprise and Education editions remain supported until October 12, 2027. For more information, see the How to get the Windows 11 2025 Update video.

For devices running Windows 10 Enterprise LTSB 2016, we recommend updating to the latest LTSC release, Windows 11 Enterprise LTSC 2024. Extended Security Updates (ESU) are also available for organizations that need additional time to complete their transition.

For more information about product lifecycle dates, see Windows 11 release information, Windows 10 release information, and Lifecycle FAQ - Windows.

title: Microsoft Viva Glint: Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 upload now available

details.name: Summary
details.value: Organizations can now upload Viva Glint organizational data via Organizational Data in Microsoft 365, enabling centralized, automated data management with supported HRIS connectors. Starting September 30, 2026, this will replace SFTP uploads for new customers. Administrators should prepare for migration to improve data consistency and governance.

body.content:
[What and Why]

Organizations can now upload Viva Glint organizational data through Organizational Data in Microsoft 365. This provides a centralized experience for ingesting, managing, and governing organizational data across Microsoft 365 and Viva applications.

This change helps improve data consistency, governance, and scalability by supporting automated data ingestion through supported HRIS connectors and reducing reliance on SFTP-based uploads. Organizational data can be reused across Microsoft 365 experiences, helping organizations maintain a single source of truth for workforce data.

[Rollout Schedule]

  • Availability: July 15, 2026
  • Beginning September 30, 2026: Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 will be the preferred method for Viva Glint organizational data ingestion. SFTP-based data ingestion will no longer be available for new Viva Glint customers.
  • General Availability (Worldwide): Beginning in mid-July 2026 and expected to complete by late September 2026

[Impact on Your Organization]

Who is affected

  • Viva Glint Service Administrators responsible for organizational data uploads
  • Microsoft 365 Global Administrators
  • Organizational Data Source Administrators
  • HRIS/HCM administrators

Platforms/Services

  • Microsoft Viva Glint
  • Organizational Data in Microsoft 365
  • Microsoft 365 admin center
  • Supported HRIS connectors (for example, Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, API-based integrations, and Azure Data Lake Storage)

What will happen

  • Organizations can upload Viva Glint organizational data through Organizational Data in Microsoft 365.
  • Organizational data can be managed through a centralized Microsoft 365 experience.
  • Automated ingestion options are available through supported HRIS and data connectors.
  • Organizations can manage access to organizational attributes and control which applications can access certain data.
  • Organizational Data Source Administrators can manage uploads and organizational data ingestion without requiring Microsoft 365 Global Administrator permissions.
  • Beginning September 30, 2026, Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 will be the preferred method for Viva Glint organizational data ingestion. SFTP-based data ingestion will no longer be available for new Viva Glint customers.
  • Organizations that are not currently using SFTP-based uploads will experience no disruption to their existing upload process. They may choose to adopt Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 to take advantage of centralized data management and automated ingestion capabilities.

[Action Required/Recommendations]

If your organization currently uses SFTP for Viva Glint uploads, complete the following steps before September 30, 2026:

  • Identify who should administer organizational data uploads. This is typically a Viva Glint Service Administrator, HRIS/HCM administrator, or Organizational Data Source Administrator.
  • Review Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 documentation and determine the most appropriate ingestion method for your organization.
  • Enable Viva Glint readiness through the onboarding experience within Viva Glint.
  • Evaluate future migration plans for organizational data ingestion.

If your organization is not currently using SFTP for Viva Glint uploads, no action is required.

Organizations may choose to adopt Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 to take advantage of centralized data management and automated ingestion capabilities.

Recommended:

  • Review how to use your organizational data in Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Viva.
  • Understand how organizational data is used and retained in Microsoft 365.
  • Review how to import and manage your organizational data with a CSV file.

Learn more

  • Consuming data from Organizational data in Microsoft 365 into Viva Glint | Viva Glint | Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Learn
  • Import and manage your organizational data with a .csv file | Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Learn
  • Import employee data from Organizational data in Microsoft 365 into Viva Glint | Viva Glint | Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Learn
  • Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 - Attribute reference | Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Learn
  • Understand how organizational data is used and retained in Microsoft 365 | Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Learn
  • Upload and maintain data through the Microsoft 365 admin center | Viva Insights | Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Learn
  • Use your organizational data in Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Viva | Microsoft Learn

[Compliance considerations]

QuestionAnswer
Does the change alter how existing customer data is processed, stored, or accessed?Yes. Organizational data can be ingested through Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 and becomes available to supported Microsoft 365 and Viva experiences.
Does the change store new customer data?Yes. Organizational data uploaded through Organizational Data in Microsoft 365 is stored and managed within Microsoft 365. Documentation states that organizational data is stored as part of the Microsoft 365 profile experience and is available to supported services.
Does the change add integration to third-party software products?Yes. Organizations may connect supported HRIS systems such as Workday and SAP SuccessFactors or use API-based integrations for automated ingestion.
Does the change include an admin control?Organizational Data Source Administrator and Microsoft 365 Global Administrator roles can manage uploads, data access, and connector configuration.

title: Microsoft Teams: Enhanced delegated calling with delegate call access restrictions and join notifications

details.name: FeatureStatusJson
details.value: {"0":[{"RoadmapId":0,"McPostId":"MC1429012","Platform":"Desktop","Status":"FeatureRolloutStatusNotSupported","LastUpdateTime":"2026-07-19T03:09:39.1825823Z","LatestRing":null,"TenantRolloutRing":null},{"RoadmapId":0,"McPostId":"MC1429012","Platform":"Mac","Status":"FeatureRolloutStatusNotSupported","LastUpdateTime":"2026-07-19T03:09:39.1825825Z","LatestRing":null,"TenantRolloutRing":null},{"RoadmapId":0,"McPostId":"MC1429012","Platform":"All","Status":"FeatureRolloutStatusNotSupported","LastUpdateTime":"2026-07-19T03:09:39.1825827Z","LatestRing":null,"TenantRolloutRing":null}]}

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft Teams is adding call delegation enhancements: delegators can lock active calls to block delegate participation and enable warning tones notifying when delegates join or resume calls. These features improve privacy and transparency during calls, rolling out worldwide from August to September 2026. No immediate admin action needed.

details.name: Platforms
details.value: Desktop, Mac

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Microsoft is enhancing Microsoft Teams call delegation with new controls that provide greater privacy, transparency, and control during active calls. Delegators will be able to lock active calls to prevent delegate participation and optionally enable warning tones that notify participants whenever a delegate joins or resumes a call. These enhancements help users manage sensitive conversations more effectively while improving awareness during delegated call handling.

This message is associated with Roadmap ID 567306.

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • Targeted Release (Worldwide): We will begin rolling out in early August 2026 and expect to complete by mid-August 2026.
  • General Availability (Worldwide): We will begin rolling out in mid-August 2026 and expect to complete by mid-August 2026.
  • General Availability (GCC, GCC High): We will begin rolling out in mid-August 2026 and expect to complete by late August 2026.
  • General Availability (DoD): We will begin rolling out in mid-August 2026 and expect to complete by early September 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Users with Microsoft Teams call delegation configured.
  • Executives, administrative assistants, managers, and support personnel who use delegated calling.

Platforms/Services:

  • Microsoft Teams
  • Teams Calling
  • Teams desktop
  • Teams web

What will happen:

  • A new Lock an Ongoing Call capability will be available to delegators.

    • Screenshot 1: During an active call, delegators can use the new Lock call control from the meeting toolbar to restrict delegate participation in sensitive conversations:

    Screenshot 2: Delegators can lock an active Teams call directly from the Calls experience, preventing delegates from joining or resuming the call until it is unlocked:

  • Delegators can lock an active call to prevent delegates from:
    • Resuming a call that has been placed on hold.
    • Joining an ongoing active call.
  • Delegates will continue to see locked calls in their call history.
  • Delegates will be unable to participate in locked calls until the delegator unlocks the call.
  • A new optional Warning Tone for Delegate Participation setting will be available.

    • Screenshot 3: New call delegation settings allow users to enable an audible notification that alerts participants whenever a delegate joins or resumes a delegated call:

  • When enabled, all call participants will hear an audible notification when a delegate:
    • Joins an active call.
    • Resumes a held call.
  • The feature increases transparency regarding delegate participation in live calls.
  • This change will be available by default to users with call delegation configured.
  • No existing call delegation workflows are removed.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

No immediate administrative action is required.

Recommended actions:

  • Review how call delegation is used within your organization.
  • Inform users who utilize delegated calling about the new lock and notification capabilities.
  • Update internal Teams calling documentation and training materials.
  • Notify help desk and support teams of the new user experience to prepare for potential questions.
  • Evaluate whether the delegate participation warning tone should be recommended for users handling confidential or sensitive conversations.
  • Monitor Microsoft's official documentation for feature configuration details as rollout approaches.

Learn more: Share a phone line with a delegate in Microsoft Teams | Microsoft Support (will be updated for GA rollout)

[Compliance Considerations:]

Compliance Question Explanation
Does the change provide a new way of communicating between users, tenants, or subscriptions? The feature introduces new delegated calling controls that affect how delegates can participate in ongoing calls. It also adds an optional audible notification that informs all call participants when a delegate joins or resumes a delegated call, increasing transparency during communications.
Does the change allow a user to enable and disable the feature themselves? Delegators can lock and unlock ongoing calls and can enable or disable the warning tone notification setting for delegate participation.

title: Microsoft Teams: AI meeting archive file generation

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft Teams will generate AI meeting archive files capturing key insights to enhance Microsoft 365 Copilot and Facilitator responses while ensuring privacy. Stored in tenant-owned SharePoint, archives are enabled by default with admin controls available before rollout in August 2026. Retention is configurable, and access is restricted to meeting participants.

details.name: Platforms
details.value: Web

body.content:
[What and Why]

Microsoft Teams is introducing AI generated meeting archive files that capture key insights from meetings. These archives help Microsoft 365 Copilot and Facilitator deliver more accurate context aware responses while maintaining strong privacy controls by not storing raw meeting content. This capability supports enterprise ready AI by improving knowledge grounding while keeping data secure and manageable.

This message is associated with Microsoft 365 Roadmap ID 558933.

[Rollout Schedule]

  • Targeted Release: Beginning early August 2026 and expected to complete late August 2026
  • General Availability (Worldwide): Beginning late August 2026 and expected to complete late September 2026

Teams admin center controls for this feature will be available approximately two weeks before archive file generation begins. The dates above reflect when admin controls become available.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected

  • Microsoft Teams administrators
  • Users with Microsoft 365 Copilot or Facilitator capabilities enabled

Platforms/Services

  • Microsoft Teams
  • Microsoft 365 Copilot
  • SharePoint Embedded

What will happen

  • AI meeting archive files (.meeting files) will be generated for meetings where AI is turned on, one participant has a Copilot license, and transcription is enabled. The feature is enabled by default in your organization.
  • Archive files are stored in a tenant-owned SharePoint Embedded container, not in user storage. You can control the default expiration time for these files within your organization.
  • Users cannot directly view or access archive files.
  • When the feature is released, Facilitator will use the archive to answer questions about meetings and responses include citations linking to meeting recaps or invites. In the future, Copilot will also use the archive to answer questions about meetings. 
  • Archive files cannot be modified, renamed, or moved.
  • All content in archive files is AI-generated. No raw transcript snippets are stored.
  • Archive generation requires all of the following:
    • A meeting transcript is created and saved
    • At least one Copilot-licensed participant is invited
    • The meeting option "Allow Copilot and Facilitator" is set to "During and after the meeting"
  • Archive files are not created for:
    • Meetings with sensitivity labels that include encryption
    • End-to-end encrypted meetings
    • Meetings with restricted copying or forwarding
  • Access and data protection:
    • Only users with access to the meeting can receive responses grounded in its archive and AI tools do not surface insights to users who do not have meeting access.
    • Archive knowledge is only shared with users who are in the same tenant as the meeting organizer.

[Action Required / Recommendations]

No immediate action is required. However, administrators should review configuration settings and consider organizational policy alignment.

Admins can enable or disable this feature in the Teams admin center.

Recommended actions:

  • Review the AI archive setting in Teams admin center before rollout. Setting path: Meeting Policies > AI Memory and Archive > "Allow AI to archive meetings with .meeting file generation".
  • Evaluate the default expiration period, which is set to five years (1,825 days), and adjust retention settings as needed to align with compliance policies.
  • Communicate this capability to helpdesk teams and compliance stakeholders.
  • Monitor availability of the AI archive setting in Teams admin center and review or update settings when they become available. Archive file generation will begin 2 weeks after the setting is released to your organization.

Learn more: AI Archives for Microsoft Teams Meetings | Microsoft Teams | Microsoft Learn 

[Compliance considerations]

QuestionAnswer
Does the change store new customer data, and if so, where, and is the data cached or permanently stored?Yes. The change introduces AI-generated meeting archive files that are stored in a tenant-owned SharePoint Embedded container and retained according to a configurable retention period.
Does the change alter how existing customer data is processed, stored, or accessed?Yes. Meeting transcripts and metadata are processed into structured AI archive files that enable downstream AI systems such as Copilot and Facilitator to reason over meeting content.
Does the change introduce or significantly modify AI or machine learning capabilities that interact with or provide access to customer data?Yes. Copilot and Facilitator use AI meeting archive files to generate context-aware responses grounded in prior meeting content.
Does the change provide users any new way of interacting with generative AI?Yes. Users can interact with Copilot and Facilitator, which leverage meeting archive data to answer questions and provide insights about meetings.
Does the change modify retention policies, holds, or deletion workflows?Yes. AI meeting archive files are governed by a default retention period of five years and are automatically deleted upon expiration.
Does the change include an admin control, and can it be controlled through Entra ID group membership?Yes. Administrators can enable or disable AI meeting archive generation and configure retention settings in the Teams Admin Center.

title: Microsoft SharePoint Online: Restricted access control for sites enforced across Microsoft 365 search

details.name: Summary
details.value: Restricted Access Control (RAC) for SharePoint and OneDrive sites will be enforced across Microsoft 365 search, limiting search results to content users can access. This update, rolling out in late July 2026, applies automatically to existing and new RAC-enabled sites without changing RAC configurations.

body.content:
[What and Why]

We are enhancing how Restricted Access Control (RAC) for sites is enforced across Microsoft 365 search by extending RAC policy enforcement to Microsoft 365 search, including SharePoint search, organization-wide search, and Microsoft 365 app experiences such as Office.com.

RAC limits access to SharePoint and OneDrive sites that have RAC enabled to specific groups. With this update, users will only see search results for content they are permitted to access based on RAC policies, improving data security and ensuring consistency between access controls and content discovery.

This update applies to both existing and new RAC-enabled SharePoint sites and OneDrive accounts.

[Rollout Schedule]

General Availability (Worldwide, GCC High, DoD): Rollout is expected to begin in late July 2026 and complete by late July 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization]

Who is affected

  • Admins managing SharePoint Online and OneDrive site access
  • All users in organizations with RAC-enabled sites

Platforms/Services

  • SharePoint Online
  • OneDrive
  • Microsoft 365 search (including SharePoint search, organization-wide search, and Microsoft 365 app experiences)

What will happen

  • RAC policies for sites will be enforced in Microsoft 365 search results.
  • This applies to both SharePoint sites and OneDrive accounts with RAC enabled.
  • This change applies to existing and new sites where RAC is applied.
  • This change is applied automatically as part of the service update.
  • There is no change to how RAC policies are configured.

[Action Required/Recommendations]

No action is required to receive this update. However, you may consider the following:

  • Review RAC configurations and associated group memberships for SharePoint and OneDrive sites.
  • Inform helpdesk and support teams about changes to search result visibility.
  • Update internal documentation that references search behavior and access expectations.

Learn more: Documentation will be updated closer to broad rollout (~90% completion).

  • Restrict OneDrive access by security group | SharePoint in Microsoft 365 | SharePoint | Microsoft Learn
  • Restrict SharePoint site access with Microsoft 365 groups and Microsoft Entra security groups | SharePoint in Microsoft 365 | SharePoint | Microsoft Learn

[Compliance considerations]

QuestionAnswer
Does the change alter how existing customer data is processed, stored, or accessed (for example, documents, emails, chats)?Yes. This update changes how existing content is surfaced in search results by enforcing existing permissions (RAC policies).

title: Microsoft Teams: Townhall media optimization for Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) attendees

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft Teams will offload Townhall audio and video streams from Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) to Windows endpoints, improving media quality and reducing virtual desktop load. Rollout starts September 2026, requires updated Teams and VDI clients, and supports existing interactivity and eCDNs without admin configuration.

details.name: Platforms
details.value: Desktop

body.content:
[Introduction]

Microsoft Teams is improving the Town hall viewing experience for attendees connecting through Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI). With this update, audio and video streams for Townhall attendees are offloaded directly to the user’s local Windows endpoint when using the new Teams VDI optimization. Offloading means that audio and video processing and delivery move from the virtual desktop to the attendee’s local device, allowing media to stream directly between the device and Microsoft Teams service endpoints over the local network instead of through the VDI session. This reduces virtual desktop resource usage while delivering smoother playback and higher-definition audio and video. This applies only to Windows endpoints.

This message is associated with Roadmap ID 561648.

[When this will happen:]

  • Targeted Release: Rollout begins in early-September 2026 and is expected to complete by mid-September 2026.
  • General Availability (Worldwide, GCC): Rollout begins late-September 2026 and is expected to complete by mid-October 2026.
  • General Availability (GCCH, DoD): Rollout begins mid-October 2026 and is expected to complete by late-October 2026.

[How this affects your organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Townhall attendees using Windows endpoints in VDI environments
  • Organizations using the new Microsoft Teams VDI optimization
  • Supported platforms: Azure Virtual Desktop and Windows 365, Citrix, Omnissa, Amazon WorkSpaces

What will happen:

  • Audio and video for Town hall events are streamed directly to the attendee’s local device instead of through the virtual desktop.
  • Users receive:
    • Improved video quality (HD where available)
    • Lower latency and more reliable playback
    • Reduced performance impact on the virtual desktop session
  • All existing attendee interactivity features remain available, including:
    • Live captions
    • DVR
    • Reactions
    • Streaming chat
    • Q&A
  • Both first‑party and third‑party enterprise CDNs (eCDNs) are supported.
  • When eCDNs are enabled, the attendee’s endpoint establishes a direct peer‑to‑peer connection over the local network, avoiding virtual desktop “double hop” media traffic.
  • This feature is enabled automatically when the required Teams client and VDI components are updated; no new admin policy configuration is required.

[What you can do to prepare:]

  • Plan to upgrade to a future Teams version targeted for mid-August 2026 (26211.x.x.x or higher)
  • Ensure your VDI platform components are updated to supported versions:
    • Citrix plugin for Windows: version 2026.15.1.1
    • Windows App for Windows (AVD/Windows 365): 2.0.1186.0
    • Omnissa Horizon Client for Windows: upcoming release planned for mid-2026 (version 2606 / 8.19 or higher)
    • Amazon WorkSpaces client for Windows: 5.33.0
  • Inform helpdesk and VDI administrators that media traffic for Town hall attendees will shift from the virtual desktop to the local endpoint.
  • Review internal network policies and bandwidth to ensure local endpoint media traffic is allowed for Teams.
  • If using third party eCDNs, contact your provider to review any potential configuration change required.

Learn more: Town halls and live events | Microsoft Learn

[Compliance considerations:]

No compliance considerations identified, review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Dynamics 365 Customer Service and Contact Center - New "After Conversation Work" presence for representatives in wrap-up

body.content:
We are introducing the "After Conversation Work" status to the presence feature in Dynamics 365 Customer Service and Dynamics 365 Contact Center. This feature enhancement will be available starting July 15, 2026.

How does this affect me?
This feature enables supervisors to clearly distinguish representatives who are performing after-conversation (wrap-up) work from those who are otherwise Busy or in Do Not Disturb using two new custom presences available in the Copilot Service admin center. These new presences, located under Productivity > Custom presence > Manage > Active Presences, are:
  • Busy - After Conversation Work - Indicates that the representative is in the Busy state and working on after-conversation (wrap-up) work.
  • Do Not Disturb - After Conversation Work - Indicates that the representative is in the Do Not Disturb (DND) state and working on after-conversation (wrap-up) work.
What action do I need to take?
No action is required to enable the new presences. For the "After Conversation Work" presence to take effect, ensure that "Wrap-up" is configured to block capacity for your service representatives. You can review the available custom presences in the Copilot Service admin center under Productivity > Custom presence > Manage.

title: Dynamics 365 Finance – Delay settlement from journal posting

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to delay settlements from journal posting for Dynamics 365 Finance. This feature will reach general availability on August 17, 2026.

How does this affect me?
This feature includes the following capabilities:
  • Payments are no longer blocked by settlement errors: Valid payments post successfully, even if settlement fails.
  • Enhanced cash flow visibility and reporting: Payments are reflected immediately, while settlements can be managed separately.
  • Streamlined exception handling: Failed settlements are isolated and can be retried or managed independently.
What do I need to do to prepare?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

For more information on this feature, please visit Delay settlement from journal posting.

title: Dynamics 365 Finance - Enhancements to fixed assets

body.content:
We are announcing enhancements to fixed assets in Dynamics 365 Finance. These features will reach public preview on July 31, 2026.

How does this affect me?
This update brings the following features to enhance fixed assets:

Fixed asset intercompany transfer - The intercompany transfer feature lets you transfer assets between legal entities. Select the source asset and its book, and preview key financial details like acquisition cost, accumulated depreciation, and net book value. You can process the transfer as a disposal. Choose to create the destination asset automatically or select existing assets. The feature copies asset data to the destination entity, lets you preview destination values, and automates posting acquisition in the destination asset and disposal transactions in the source asset.

Mid-month intercompany transfer support - This feature transfers the fixed asset across legal entities by supporting an accurate asset transfer on any date of the month.
  • Transfer wizard calculates prorated depreciation from the last depreciation date to the transfer date.
  • System posts the necessary depreciation adjustment automatically before processing the transfer.
New transfer value methods - This feature provides flexible valuation options for intercompany transfers.
  • Introduces new value as the third transfer value method besides acquisition and depreciation and net book value.
  • Supports automatic profit and loss calculation based on new value of the asset transfer.
Redesigned in-legal-entity split experience - This feature delivers an improved, scalable framework for splitting assets.
  • Adds a split mapping framework for consistent rules across asset groups and books.
  • New split wizard supports multi asset selection, destination setup, preview, and journal creation.
  • Enables split by percentage, amount, or quantity, with options for spot or historical exchange rates and before and after previews.
Enhancements for reducing balance depreciation - This feature ensures accurate depreciation for migrated and transferred assets using reducing balance methods.
  • Adds logic to capture or derive prior year ending net book value to establish the correct depreciation base.
  • Supports accurate depreciation for opening balance migration and mid year intercompany transfers.
What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

If you would like more information on this feature, please visit Enhancements to fixed assets.

title: Dynamics 365 Project Operations - Manage exchange rate variances in cash advance transactions

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to manage exchange rate variances in cash advance transactions in Dynamics 365 Project Operations. This feature will be available in public preview on July 31, 2026.

How does this affect me?
Users will be able to detect, calculate, and post realized foreign exchange rate variances when cash advances are issued in one currency and settled in another currency or on a different date. This feature will:
  • Base cash advance settlements on the transaction currency.
  • Calculate and post realized foreign exchange gains or losses.
  • Ensure employee accounts net to zero after settlement.
  • Improve visibility into exchange rate variances for financial reconciliation and auditing.
What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

If you would like more information on this feature, please visit Manage exchange rate variances in cash advance transactions.

title: Dynamics 365 Customer Service - Next Best Actions with Customer Intent Agent

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to select the next best actions (NBA) with Customer Intent Agent In Dynamics 365 Customer Service. This feature will reach general availability on July 31, 2026.

How does this affect me?
This new feature allows customer service representatives (CSRs) to navigate to the Copilot pane and perform a suite of actions powered by Copilot in one click.

This list of actions includes but is not limited to:
  • Drafting emails.
  • Updating knowledge articles and case notes.
This feature is admin controlled and can be turned on for the whole organization from the Customer Service admin center. Turning on NBA populates the actions in the Copilot pane for the CSR to use. Every important attribute about the case is collated in one area as the CSR gains information from back-and-forth exchange with customers

Knowledge articles are provided if CSRs are curious to dig deeper and see the reasoning behind suggestions given.

What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

title: Finance and operations apps - Use Copilot Cowork to orchestrate insights and actions across Dynamics 365 ERP

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to use Copilot Cowork to orchestrate insights and actions across Dynamics 365 ERP. This feature will reach public preview on July 31, 2026.

How does this affect me?
This feature enables users to move from business intent to insight to execution in a single experience. Copilot orchestrates work across ERP data, emails, documents, spreadsheets, collaboration tools, and downstream business processes.

Key capabilities of this feature include the ability to:
  • Orchestrate workflows across ERP and Microsoft 365 data sources.
  • Analyze business data and generate contextual insights.
  • Reason across finance, operations, and supply chain domains.
  • Generate recommendations and prepare next steps.
  • Execute actions while keeping humans in the loop.
What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

If you would like more information on this feature, please visit Use Copilot Cowork to orchestrate insights and actions across Dynamics 365 ERP.

title: Power Automate - Visualize desktop flows with flow chart view

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to visualize desktop flows with flow chart view in Power Automate. This feature will be available in public preview on July 31, 2026.

How does this affect me?
Users will be able to switch between the existing linear action list and a visual flow chart view while editing desktop flows. The flow chart view will display actions as connected nodes, making branching, dependencies, and execution paths easier to understand. Users will also be able to rearrange actions by dragging nodes and continue editing in either view, which will improve readability and troubleshooting for complex desktop flows.

What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

If you would like more information on this feature, please visit Visualize desktop flows with flow chart view.

title: Dynamics 365 Sales - Teams owned opportunities to be researched by Sales Opportunity Agent

body.content:
We are announcing the ability to utilize the Sales Opportunity Agent to research opportunities owned by specific Owner teams in Dynamics 365 Sales. This feature will reach general availability on July 31, 2026.

How does this affect me?
As a seller, you will be able to see Sales Opportunity Agent research results for opportunities owned by Owner teams. As an admin, you can select a selection of criteria that encompasses opportunities that are not owned by individuals but rather owned by Owner teams.

What action do I need to take?
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.

title: 90-Day Reminder: Windows Server 2022 will reach end of mainstream support on October 13, 2026

body.content:
On October 13, 2026, Windows Server 2022 will reach end of mainstream support. The October 2026 security update will be the last mainstream support update available for this version. After this date, Windows Server 2022 will transition to extended support, which includes security updates at no additional cost, and will continue to receive monthly security updates through October 14, 2031. For more information, see the Fixed Lifecycle Policy.

Windows Server 2025 is now the latest Long-Term Servicing Channel (LTSC) release for Windows Server. To help keep your environment protected and supported, plan to upgrade to Windows Server 2025 for full mainstream support. To download a free 180-day evaluation, visit the Microsoft Evaluation Center. Evaluate upgrade options and begin deployment testing early to help ensure a smooth transition.

For detailed information, see the Windows Server 2022 lifecycle page. For information about servicing timelines and lifecycle, see Windows Server release information, Lifecycle FAQ - Windows Server, and Microsoft Lifecycle Policy search tool.

title: Microsoft Teams: Enhanced file filtering in channel Shared tabs

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft Teams now offers enhanced file filtering in the channel Shared tab, allowing users to filter files by type and media content in both All Documents and In Messages views. This update improves file discovery without requiring admin action and is available worldwide.

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Microsoft is enhancing file discovery within Microsoft Teams channels by adding new filtering capabilities to the Shared tab. This update responds directly to customer feedback requesting easier ways to locate channel files and message attachments. The new filters improve productivity by helping users quickly find relevant content based on file type and media content, reducing time spent searching across channel resources.

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • Worldwide, GCC, GCC High, DoD: Available now

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected: All Microsoft 365 users who access files in Teams channels.

Platforms/Services: Microsoft Teams desktop and web.

What will happen:

  • The In Messages experience will be presented as a separate view alongside All Documents.
  • Users will be able to filter channel files by file type within the All Documents view.
  • Users will be able to filter files by file type within the In Messages view.
  • A new Media filter will be available within the In Messages view.
  • The feature will be enabled automatically as part of the service update.
  • No policy or configuration changes are required.
  • Existing file permissions and access controls remain unchanged.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

No administrative action is required.

Recommended actions:

  • Review internal Teams training and documentation for file management workflows.
  • Notify helpdesk and support teams of the updated experience.
  • Communicate the change to users before rollout completion.
  • Update any internal screenshots or user guides that reference the current Shared tab experience.

[Compliance Considerations:]

No compliance considerations identified, review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Teams frontline mobile onboarding guide retirement

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft is retiring the Frontline Teams Personal Device Onboarding Wizard by late August 2026. Frontline workers will use existing Microsoft Teams, Entra ID, Intune, and Authenticator onboarding methods. No changes to security controls are needed. Organizations should update documentation and training accordingly.

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Microsoft is retiring the Frontline Teams Personal Device Onboarding Wizard, a web-based experience that helped frontline workers configure Microsoft Teams on their personal Android and iOS devices. Users will continue to onboard through existing Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft Intune, and Microsoft Authenticator experiences. Existing security controls such as MFA, app protection policies, and Conditional Access remain unchanged.

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • Retirement begins in mid-August 2026 and completes by late August 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Organizations using the Frontline Teams Personal Device Onboarding Wizard.
  • Administrators managing frontline worker onboarding and BYOD deployments.
  • Helpdesk teams and frontline managers.

What will happen:

  • The Frontline Teams Personal Device Onboarding Wizard will be retired and will no longer be available to frontline workers on personal Android and iOS devices.
  • Frontline workers will continue to be able to install and sign in to Microsoft Teams using existing Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Entra ID, Intune, and Microsoft Authenticator onboarding experiences, as applicable to your organization's requirements.
  • No new admin controls or configuration changes are required as part of this retirement.

This change does not affect:

  • Microsoft Teams access on personal devices.
  • Existing Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) deployments.
  • Multi-factor authentication (MFA).
  • App protection policies.
  • Conditional Access policies.

Only the dedicated onboarding wizard experience is being retired.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

Action is recommended if your organization currently references this onboarding wizard.

Recommended administrator checklist:

  • Review onboarding documentation for references to the Frontline Teams Personal Device Onboarding Wizard.
  • Update BYOD deployment guides and frontline worker training materials.
  • Create or update alternative instructions for:
    • Installing Microsoft Teams on Android and iOS devices.
    • Signing in to Microsoft Teams.
    • Completing MFA registration requirements.
    • Completing Intune enrollment or app protection requirements where applicable.
  • Communicate the retirement to helpdesk personnel and frontline managers before mid-August 2026.
  • Validate that existing onboarding documentation points users to Microsoft Teams, Microsoft Entra ID, Microsoft Intune, and Microsoft Authenticator experiences rather than the retired wizard.
  • Review support scripts and knowledge articles to ensure they do not reference the retired experience.

[Compliance Considerations:]

No compliance considerations identified, review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Microsoft OneDrive: Exclude specific folders from syncing

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft OneDrive will allow administrators to exclude specific folders (e.g., node_modules, PowerShell modules) from syncing, keeping them local to devices. This feature, off by default, rolls out worldwide in August 2026, improves storage efficiency, and is managed via Group Policy with no user control over exclusions.

details.name: Platforms
details.value: Desktop, Mac

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Microsoft is introducing a new OneDrive capability that allows organizations to exclude specific folders from syncing to OneDrive. This helps ensure machine-specific or development-related folders—such as PowerShell modules, Visual Studio configuration folders, and node_modules—remain local to the device instead of being uploaded to the cloud.

This enhancement improves manageability and storage efficiency by giving administrators granular control over which folders can be synchronized. It supports enterprise-ready management scenarios by helping organizations prevent unnecessary or device-specific content from consuming cloud storage while maintaining a consistent OneDrive experience for users.

This message is associated with Roadmap ID 567470.

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • General Availability (Worldwide): We will begin rolling out in mid-August 2026 and expect to complete by late August 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Organizations using OneDrive for Windows and Mac
  • OneDrive administrators managing sync policies
  • Users storing development or machine-specific folders within OneDrive-managed locations

Platforms/Services:

  • OneDrive for Windows
  • OneDrive for Mac
  • OneDrive Sync Client
  • Group Policy

What will happen:

  • The feature is OFF by default.
  • No changes occur unless administrators configure exclusion rules.
  • Administrators can define folder names or path patterns that should not sync to OneDrive.
  • Folders matching configured rules remain on the local device and are not uploaded to OneDrive.
  • Contents of excluded folders are also excluded from synchronization.
  • Existing folders already synchronized before a rule is applied continue syncing until the user moves them out of OneDrive and then back into a synchronized location.
  • Local files moved into an excluded folder after policy enforcement will remain local and will be excluded from syncing to OneDrive cloud storage. Moving files that are already syncing into an excluded folder is currently not supported.
  • OneDrive must be restarted after policy changes for updated rules to take effect.
  • Common use cases include excluding:
    • node_modules
    • PowerShell module directories
    • Visual Studio configuration folders
    • Other machine-specific development tooling folders

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

Action Required: Only if you plan to use this feature.

Recommended checklist:

  • Review whether your organization has local-only folders that should be excluded from OneDrive synchronization.
  • Evaluate development, scripting, or application configuration folders that generate unnecessary cloud storage consumption.
  • Plan and implement folder exclusion rules using the new Group Policy settings when they become available.
  • Test exclusion rules with a pilot group before broad deployment.
  • Restart OneDrive after deploying or modifying exclusion policies to ensure policy enforcement.
  • Update internal user guidance explaining which folders will remain local-only.
  • Inform help desk and support personnel about the new behavior and expected user experience.
  • Document any excluded folders within your organization's endpoint management standards.

If you do not intend to use the feature:

  • No action is required.
  • Sync behavior remains unchanged unless exclusion policies are configured.

Learn more: IT Admins - Use OneDrive policies to control sync settings | Microsoft Learn (will be updated before rollout)

[Compliance Considerations:]

Consideration Explanation
Does the change alter how existing customer data is processed, stored, or accessed? When administrators configure folder exclusion rules, matching folders and their contents are no longer synchronized to OneDrive and remain stored locally on the device. Files moved into excluded folders after policy enforcement are removed from OneDrive cloud storage.
Does the change include an admin control and can it be controlled through Entra ID group membership? Administrators can configure folder exclusion rules through Group Policy settings that accompany this feature. The announcement confirms administrative control of the functionality.
Does the change allow a user to enable and disable the feature themselves? Users cannot create or modify exclusion rules, but they must restart OneDrive for policy changes to take effect on their device. Feature enablement remains under administrator control.

title: Microsoft Outlook: Meeting Insight is retiring

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft is retiring Outlook's Meeting Insights by early September 2026, replacing it with Microsoft 365 Copilot's meeting preparation features for licensed users. Meeting Insights will be removed across platforms, with no action required but recommended communication, documentation updates, and user training. No compliance issues noted.

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Microsoft is retiring Meeting Insights, the feature that surfaces relevant emails and files within meeting details. To provide a more intelligent and context-aware meeting preparation experience, users with a Microsoft 365 Copilot license can instead use Copilot meeting preparation experiences that summarize relevant content, tasks, and context available to the user.

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • Worldwide, GCC, GCC High: Retirement begins mid-August 2026; expected to complete by early September 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Users who currently use Meeting Insights.
  • Organizations with Meeting Insights enabled.
  • Microsoft 365 Copilot licensed users who will use the replacement experience.

Platforms/Services:

  • Outlook on the web
  • New Outlook for Windows
  • Outlook Mobile
  • Outlook for Mac
  • Microsoft Teams
  • Microsoft 365 Copilot

What will happen:

  • Meeting Insights will be removed from meeting event details.
  • Users will no longer see relevant files and emails surfaced through Meeting Insights.
  • Users with a Microsoft 365 Copilot license can use the "Prepare for this meeting" experience.
  • Copilot will summarize relevant meeting context, tasks, and documents available to the user.
  • Meeting preparation content remains permission-trimmed based on user access.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

No action is required.

We recommend that you:

  • Communicate this change to end users and help desk teams.
  • Update internal documentation that references Meeting Insights.
  • Review Microsoft documentation for the replacement Copilot experience.
  • Provide user training for Copilot meeting preparation experiences if applicable.

[Compliance Considerations:]

No compliance considerations identified, review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Copilot in Excel: Brand Kit skill

details.name: Summary
details.value: Copilot in Excel introduces a Brand kit skill that applies organizational branding from Microsoft 365 Brand Center to workbook tables and charts, ensuring consistent, professional reports. Rolling out worldwide from mid-July to mid-August 2026, it requires no action and enhances presentation without altering data.

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Copilot in Excel is introducing a new Brand kit skill that applies your organization's approved branding directly to workbook content. Using brand assets from Microsoft 365 Brand Center, Copilot can automatically format tables and charts with approved colors, fonts, and logos, helping users create consistent, professional reports without manual styling. This enhancement supports Personal and Team Productivity by making it easier to produce presentation-ready content that aligns with organizational branding standards.

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • Targeted Release (Worldwide): Rollout begins mid-July 2026 and is expected to complete by late July 2026.
  • General Availability (Worldwide): Rollout begins mid-July 2026 and is expected to complete by mid-August 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Users with access to Copilot in Excel.
  • Organizations that have configured branding assets in Microsoft 365 Brand Center.

Platforms/Services:

  • Microsoft Excel
  • Microsoft 365 Copilot in Excel
  • Microsoft 365 Brand Center

What will happen:

  • Users can ask Copilot to apply organizational branding to workbook content.
  • Copilot will use approved brand assets from Microsoft 365 Brand Center.
  • Tables and charts may be updated with approved colors, fonts, and logos.

    • Screenshot: Example of a workbook formatted using an organization's Brand kit in Copilot for Excel:

  • Users can create reports that align with corporate branding standards more quickly.
  • The feature is available through Copilot in Excel and does not require a separate deployment.
  • Workbook data is not functionally changed; the feature focuses on presentation and branding.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

No action is required.

Recommended activities:

  • Review branding assets configured in Microsoft 365 Brand Center.
  • Inform users that Copilot can apply approved organizational branding to Excel content.
  • Update Copilot adoption and training materials, as appropriate.
  • Monitor user feedback during rollout.

Learn more: Copilot in Excel skills | Microsoft Support (will be updated soon)

[Compliance considerations:]

Consideration Explanation
Does the change introduce or significantly modify AI/ML or agent capabilities that interact with or provide access to customer data? The Brand kit skill uses Copilot to analyze workbook content and apply branding elements from the organization's Microsoft 365 Brand Center assets to worksheet content.
Does the change alter how existing customer data is processed, stored, or accessed? Copilot processes workbook content to determine how approved branding elements should be applied to tables, charts, and other visual components.
Does the change provide end users any new way of interacting with generative AI? Users can use natural language prompts to request that Copilot apply their organization's brand standards to workbook content.

title: Find your group sites faster with the new My Sites tab in OneDrive for iOS

details.name: Summary
details.value: OneDrive for iOS will add a new My Sites tab by mid-July 2026, showing all group-connected SharePoint sites users own or belong to. This enables easier access to team content without searching or recent activity, with no admin action or permission changes required.

body.content:
[What and Why:]

Microsoft is introducing a new My Sites tab in the OneDrive for iOS app that provides users with a centralized view of all group-connected SharePoint sites they own or belong to. This enhancement improves personal and team productivity by making it easier to discover and access team content without relying on recent activity, search, or remembering site names. Users can quickly navigate to site document libraries directly from their iPhone or iPad, helping reduce time spent locating content and improving access to newly joined sites.

[Rollout Schedule:]

  • General Availability (Worldwide): Rollout begins in mid-July 2026 and is expected to complete by August 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization:]

Who is affected:

  • Users who access OneDrive on iOS (iPhone and iPad).
  • Users who are members or owners of group-connected SharePoint sites.
  • Microsoft 365 tenants using SharePoint Online and OneDrive.

Platforms/Services:

  • OneDrive for iOS
  • SharePoint Online
  • Microsoft 365

What will happen:

  • Users will see a new My Sites tab within the OneDrive iOS application.

  • The tab will display all group-connected SharePoint sites where the user is an owner or member.
  • Users can browse sites even if they have not recently accessed them.
  • Newly added site memberships will become easier to discover.
  • Selecting a site will open its default document library.
  • The feature is enabled by default as part of the OneDrive iOS experience.
  • No changes are being made to SharePoint site permissions or membership management.
  • No tenant-level configuration or policy changes are required.

[Action Required/Recommendations:]

No admin action is required.

We recommend that you:

  • Review the updated user experience before rollout.
  • Inform iOS users about the new My Sites navigation experience.
  • Update internal OneDrive or SharePoint training materials if applicable.
  • Prepare help desk teams to answer questions regarding the new location for accessing SharePoint sites.

[Compliance Considerations:]

No compliance considerations identified, review as appropriate for your organization.

title: Microsoft Outlook: Collaborate with Copilot while drafting email

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft Outlook will integrate Microsoft 365 Copilot into the email compose experience by mid-July 2026, enabling licensed users to create and edit emails with AI assistance in real time across multiple platforms. The feature is enabled by default, requires no admin changes, and maintains existing policies.

details.name: Platforms
details.value: Web

body.content:
[What and Why]

We are enhancing the email drafting experience in Microsoft Outlook by integrating Microsoft 365 Copilot directly into the compose experience. This update allows users to create, refine, and edit email content in real time with Copilot assistance, helping improve productivity while maintaining full control over final content.

This message is associated with Microsoft 365 Roadmap ID 564605.

[Rollout Schedule]

General Availability (Worldwide): Beginning mid-July 2026 and expected to complete by early August 2026

[Impact on Your Organization]

Who is affected

  • Users with a Microsoft 365 Copilot license who use Outlook on supported clients

Platforms/Services

  • Outlook on the web
  • New Outlook for Windows
  • Outlook mobile
  • Outlook for Mac, available after initial rollout
  • Classic Outlook for Windows, planned later in 2026

What will happen

  • Users will see a Copilot side-by-side drafting experience when composing email.
  • Users can create new messages or edit existing drafts with Copilot assistance:

  • Users can refine, expand, or rewrite content directly within the compose surface.
  • The feature is enabled by default for licensed users.
  • Existing policies for email access, governance, and protection continue to apply.
  • No changes to admin controls at this time.
  • Existing workflows outside of the compose experience are not impacted.

[Action Required/Recommendations]

No action is required.

You may consider the following:

  • Update internal training materials to reflect the new drafting experience.
  • Inform helpdesk teams of the updated compose experience.
  • Review Microsoft Learn documentation for Microsoft 365 Copilot in Outlook.

Learn more: Draft an email message with Copilot in Outlook | Microsoft Support

[Compliance considerations]

QuestionAnswer
Does the change introduce or significantly modify AI or machine learning capabilities that interact with customer data, and if so, what are the changes?Yes. The change introduces Microsoft 365 Copilot capabilities within the Outlook compose experience, allowing users to generate, refine, and edit email content using AI that interacts with customer data such as email drafts.
Does the change provide users with a new way of interacting with generative AI, and if so, how?Yes. Users can now interact directly with Microsoft 365 Copilot within the Outlook email compose surface to create and modify email content in real time.

title: Microsoft Excel: Summarize text columns and generate categories with Copilot

details.name: Summary
details.value: Microsoft 365 Copilot in Excel will automatically analyze unstructured text columns to generate categories and tags, aiding data analysis like survey responses. Rolling out worldwide mid to late July 2026, it requires no setup, affects licensed users, and maintains existing data compliance and controls.

body.content:
[What and Why]

We are introducing a new Microsoft 365 Copilot capability in Excel that helps users analyze columns of unstructured text and automatically generate categories or tags for each entry. This feature helps users quickly identify themes and patterns in data such as survey responses or customer feedback, improving productivity and reducing manual analysis effort.

[Rollout Schedule]

General Availability (Worldwide): Rollout will begin in mid-July 2026 and is expected to complete by late July 2026.

[Impact on Your Organization]

Who is affected

  • Users with a Microsoft 365 Copilot license using Excel

Platforms and services

  • Excel for Microsoft 365
